notes_and_typos: 1. Code 208276 appears TWICE in the catalogue:
- Page 49 as KERBMARKER S/STEEL 'E' 38mm x 1.6mm
- Page 52 as SPRING WATER – 600ml (EACH / CARTON 24)
One of these is a typo — verify with Stores before relying on either.
2. Sign codes 207382 and 207383 both carry description "W1-1B TURN RIGHT ARROW"
on page 44. Likely one should be W1-1B LEFT. Verify with Stores.
3. Steel Blue Penetration-Resistant boots (page 62) list codes 207141, 207142,
envelopes in the Envelopes & Letter Head section. These are almost certainly
typos for 208141-208144. Treat cautiously; verify context when coding.
4. Code "1699172" (MULTIGRIPS 250mm, page 23) has 7 digits — all other stores
codes are 6 digits. Likely a catalogue typo for 169172 or similar.
5. Code 207655 (PUMP NOZZLE TO SUIT 5L BOTTLE) appears in both HAND SOAP and
HAND SANITISER GEL sections — it's a shared accessory, not a duplicate code.
- Items drawn from Marsden Stores typically flow via internal ledger (stores
issue), NOT through AP. If a stores code appears on an external AP invoice,
this usually means the supplier is restocking Stores (e.g. deliveries from
the vendor direct to Marsden Depot) — still an AP transaction but destined
for the stores inventory account, not an end-user section cost centre.
- Natural-account coding for stores-restock invoices depends on what the
supplier is restocking, not who ultimately draws it. Most restocking flows
against stores inventory accounts; some direct-issue items may hit the
consumable category most closely matching their use (e.g. 62125
office/admin consumables for tissues/envelopes; 72111 materials for
concrete/tools/signs; 73xxx for fleet/plant consumables if site-issued).
- Evidence standard still applies: match the invoice line-item description
to the catalogue entry here to confirm category — don't infer from vendor
name alone.
usage_notes: Items drawn from Marsden Stores typically flow via internal ledger (stores issue), NOT through AP. If a stores code appears on an external AP invoice, the supplier is restocking Stores. Restocking flows against stores inventory accounts; direct-issue items may hit the consumable NA matching their use (72111 materials for concrete/tools/signs; 73xxx for fleet/plant consumables if site-issued). Evidence standard still applies: match invoice line-item description to the catalogue entry here, don't infer from vendor name alone.
